questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Let $\mathrm{I}_1=\int_0^1 \frac{\mathrm{e}^{\mathrm{x}}}{1+\mathrm{x}} \mathrm{dx}$ and $\mathrm{I}_2=\int_0^1 \frac{\mathrm{x}^2}{\mathrm{e}^{\mathrm{x}^3}\left(2-\mathrm{x}^3\right)} \mathrm{dx}$. The $\frac{\mathrm{I}_1}{\mathrm{I}_2 \mathrm{e}}$ is equal to ______
questions.solution:
Let $\mathrm{t}=\mathrm{x}^3$ in $\mathrm{I}_2$
We have $I_2=\frac{1}{3} \int_0^1 \frac{1}{e^t(2-t)} d t=\frac{1}{3} \int_0^1 \frac{1}{e^{1-t}(1+t)} d t$
$\Rightarrow \mathrm{I}_2=\frac{1}{3 \mathrm{e}} \int_0^1 \frac{\mathrm{e}^{\mathrm{t}}}{(1+\mathrm{t})} \mathrm{dt}=\frac{1}{3 \mathrm{e}} \mathrm{l}_1 \Rightarrow \frac{\mathrm{I}_1}{\mathrm{el}_2}=3$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Let $f(x)=x^3+3 x^2-6 x+11, g(x)=\ln |x|$. If $A=f\left(g^{\prime}(-1)\right), B=g\left(f^{\prime}(-1), C=\int_{-2}^1 f^{\prime}(x) g^{\prime}(f(x)) d x\right.$, $D=\frac{d}{d t} \int_1^{t^2} g(x) d x$ evaluated at $t=3$ and $A(B+C)-D=k \ln (3)$ then the value of $k$ is ___________
questions.solution:
$\begin{aligned} & A=f(-1)=19 \\ & B=g(-9)=2 \ln 3 \\ & C=\int_{-2}^1 \frac{3\left(x^2+2 x-2\right)}{x^3+3 x^2-6 x+11} d x=\ln 9-\ln 27=-\ln 3\end{aligned}$
$\begin{aligned} & D=\frac{d}{d t} \int_1^{t^2} g(x) d x=6 g(9)=12 \ln 3 \\ & A(B+C)-D=7 \ln 3 \Rightarrow k=7\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: The value of $\frac{8 \sqrt{2}}{\pi} \int_0^1\left(\frac{1-\mathrm{x}^2}{1+\mathrm{x}^2}\right) \frac{\mathrm{dx}}{\sqrt{1+\mathrm{x}^4}}$ is __________ .
questions.solution:
Let $\cos \theta=\frac{\sqrt{1+\mathrm{x}^4}}{1+\mathrm{x}^2}$, we get
$\begin{aligned}
& -\sin \theta \frac{d \theta}{d x}=\frac{2 x^3}{\left(1+x^2\right)\left(1+x^4\right)}-\frac{2 x \sqrt{1+x^4}}{\left(1+x^2\right)^2}=\frac{2 x^3\left(1+x^2\right)-2 x\left(1+x^4\right)}{\left(1+x^2\right)^2 \sqrt{1+x^4}} \\
& =\frac{-2 x\left(1-x^2\right)}{\left(1+x^2\right)^2 \sqrt{1+x^4}}=\frac{1-x^2}{\left(1+x^2\right) \sqrt{1+x^4}} d x=\left(\frac{1+x^2}{2 x}\right) \sin \theta d \theta
\end{aligned}$
The factor on the right can be put in terms of $\theta$ as follows
$\begin{aligned}
& \frac{1+x^2}{2 x}=\frac{1+x^4+2 x^2}{4 x^2}=\left(\frac{\sqrt{1+x^4}}{2 x}\right)^2+\frac{1}{2}=\left(\left(\frac{1+x^2}{2 x}\right) \cos \theta\right)^2+\frac{1}{2} \\
& \Rightarrow\left(1-\cos ^2 \theta\right)\left(\frac{1+x^2}{2 x}\right)^2=\frac{1}{2} \Rightarrow \frac{1+x^2}{2 x}=\frac{1}{\sqrt{2} \sin \theta}
\end{aligned}$
$\therefore$ the given integral becomes
$\frac{8 \sqrt{2}}{\pi} \int_0^{\pi / 4} \frac{\sin \theta d \theta}{\sqrt{2} \sin \theta}=\frac{8 \sqrt{2}}{\pi} \cdot \frac{1}{\sqrt{2}} \int_0^{\pi / 4} d \theta=\frac{8 \sqrt{2}}{\pi} \cdot \frac{1}{\sqrt{2}} \frac{\pi}{4}=2$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: The value of $\int_0^1 x \log x d x=-\frac{1}{k}$, then $k$ is __________.
questions.solution: $\int_0^1 x \log x d x=\int_0^1 \log x \cdot x d x=\left|\log x \cdot \frac{x^2}{2}\right|_0^1-\int_0^1 \frac{1}{x} \cdot \frac{x^2}{2} d x=-\frac{1}{4}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: If $I_n=\int_0^{\infty} e^{-x}(\sin x)^n d x(n\gt1)$, then the value of $\frac{101 I_{10}}{10 I_8}$ is equal to $\qquad$
questions.solution:
$I_n=\left(\frac{e^{-x}(\sin x)^n}{-1}\right)_0^{\infty}+\int_0^{\infty} n(\sin x)^{n-1} \cos x e^{-x} d x$
$=0+n\left(\frac{(\sin x)^{n-1} \cos x e^{-x}}{-1}\right)_0^{\infty}+n \int_0^{\infty}\left[(\sin x)^{n-1}(-\sin x)+(\cos x)(n-1) \cos x(\sin x)^{n-2} \cos x\right] e^{-x} d x$
$=n \int_0^{\infty}\left(-(\sin x)^n+(n-1)\left(1-\sin ^2 x\right)(\sin x)^{n-2}\right) e^{-x} d x=\frac{n(n-1)}{n^2+1} I_{n-2}$
$101 \frac{\mathrm{I}_{10}}{\mathrm{I}_8}=101 \times \frac{10 \times 9}{10^2+1}=90$.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: For positive $t$, let $\alpha, \beta$ be the root of the quadratic equation $x^2+t^2 x-2 t=0$. If minimum value of $\int_{-1}^2\left(\left(x+\frac{1}{\alpha^2}\right)\left(x+\frac{1}{\beta^2}+\frac{1}{\alpha \beta}\right)\right) d x$ is $\sqrt{p}+q$ where $p$ is a rational number and $q \in N$, then the value of $[p]+q$ is (where [.] denotes G.I.F)
questions.solution:
$\left(x+\frac{1}{\alpha^2}\right)\left(x+\frac{1}{\beta^2}\right)+\frac{1}{\alpha \beta}=x^2+\left(\frac{\alpha^2+\beta^2}{(\alpha \beta)^2}\right) x+\frac{1}{(\alpha \beta)^2}+\frac{1}{\alpha \beta}=x^2+\left(\frac{t^4+4 t}{4 t^2}\right) x+\frac{1}{4 t^2}-\frac{1}{2 t}$
Let $I=\int_{-1}^2 x^2 d x+\frac{3}{2}\left(\frac{t^2}{4}+\frac{1}{t}\right)+3\left(\frac{1}{4 t^2}-\frac{1}{2 t}\right)$
$\Rightarrow \frac{d \mathrm{~d}}{\mathrm{dt}}=\frac{3}{2}\left(\frac{\mathrm{t}}{2}-\frac{1}{\mathrm{t}^2}-\frac{1}{\mathrm{t}^3}+\frac{1}{\mathrm{t}^2}\right)=0 \Rightarrow \mathrm{t}^4=2 \Rightarrow t= \pm(2)^{1 / 4}$
$\Rightarrow \operatorname{Min} I(t)=3+\frac{3}{2}\left(\frac{\sqrt{2}}{4}+\frac{1}{2^{1 / 4}}+\frac{1}{2 \sqrt{2}}-\frac{1}{2^{1 / 4}}\right)=3+\frac{3}{2} \cdot \frac{\sqrt{2}}{2}=3+\sqrt{\frac{9}{8}}$
$\Rightarrow\left[\frac{9}{8}\right]+3=1+3=4$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: If $r_1, r_2$ and $r_3$ are three values of $r$ which satisfy the equation $\int_0^{\pi / 2}(\sin x+r \cos x)^3 d x-\frac{4 r}{\pi-2} \int_0^{\pi / 2} x \cos x d x=2$ then the value of $\frac{4}{7}\left(r_1^2+r_2^2+r_3^2\right)$ is equal to
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} & \mathrm{I}_1=\int_0^{\pi / 2}(\sin x+\mathrm{r} \cos x)^3 d x=\int_0^{\pi / 2} \sin ^3 x+\mathrm{r}^3 \cos ^3 \mathrm{x}+3 \mathrm{r} \sin ^2 x \cos \mathrm{x}+3 \mathrm{r}^2 \sin x \cos ^2 \mathrm{xdx} \\ & =\frac{2}{3}+\frac{2 \mathrm{r}^3}{3}+\mathrm{r}^2+\mathrm{r} \\ & \mathrm{I}_2=\int_0^{\pi / 2} \mathrm{x} \cos \mathrm{xdx}=(\mathrm{x} \sin \mathrm{x}+\cos \mathrm{x})_0^{\pi / 2}=\frac{\pi-2}{2} \\ & \mathrm{I}=\mathrm{I}_1+\frac{4 \mathrm{r}}{\pi-2} \mathrm{I}_2=2 \\ & \Rightarrow 2 \mathrm{r}^3+3 \mathrm{r}^2-3 \mathrm{r}+2=6 \\ & \Rightarrow 2 \mathrm{r}^3+3 \mathrm{r}^2-3 \mathrm{r}-4=0 \\ & \sum \mathrm{r}_1=-\frac{3}{2}, \sum \mathrm{r}_1 \mathrm{r}_2=-\frac{3}{2}, \sum \mathrm{r}_1^2=\frac{21}{4}\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: If $f(x)=\left|\begin{array}{ccc}4 \sin ^2 x & 1 & 1 \\ (1-\sin x)^2 & (2+\sin x)^2 & (1-\sin x)^2 \\ (1+\sin x)^2 & (1+\sin x)^2 & \sin ^2 x\end{array}\right|$, then evaluate $\frac{\int_{-\pi / 2}^{\pi / 2} f(x) d x}{-\pi}$
questions.solution:
Applying $C_1 \rightarrow C_1-C_3$ and $C_2 \rightarrow C_2-C_3$, we get
$\begin{aligned}
& f(x)=\left|\begin{array}{ccc}
4 \sin ^2 x-1 & 0 & 1 \\
0 & 3(2 \sin x+1) & (1-\sin x)^2 \\
(2 \sin x+1) & (2 \sin x+1) & \sin ^2 x
\end{array}\right| \\
& =(2 \sin x+1)^2\left|\begin{array}{ccc}
2 \sin x-1 & 0 & 1 \\
0 & 3 & (1-\sin x)^2 \\
1 & 1 & \sin ^2 x
\end{array}\right| \\
& =-16 \sin ^3 x \cos ^2 x-24 \sin ^2 x \cos ^2 x-12 \sin x \cos ^2 x-2 \cos ^2 x \\
& \therefore \\
& =\int_{-\pi / 2}^{\pi / 2} f(x) d x \\
& =\int_{-\pi / 2}^{\pi / 2}\left(-24 \sin ^2 x \cos ^2 x-2 \cos ^2 x\right) d x=-4 \pi
\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q:
Let $y=f(x)$ be a differentiable function such that $f(x)=\int_0^x\left(\frac{f(t)}{t}+\ln t\right) d t \forall x>0$ and $f(1)=0$. If $L=\lim _{x \rightarrow 1} \frac{f(x)}{\sin ^2 \pi x}$, then find the value of $\left[\frac{1}{\pi L}\right]$.
[Note: Where [ $\cdot]$ denotes greatest integer function.]
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} \quad f^{\prime}(x) & =\frac{f(x)}{x}+\ln x \Rightarrow f(x) \cdot \frac{1}{x}=\int \frac{\ln x}{x} d x+C \\ \Rightarrow \quad \frac{f(x)}{x} & =\frac{(\ln x)^2}{2}+C \Rightarrow f(x)=\frac{x(\ln x)^2}{2} \\ L & =\lim _{x \rightarrow 1} \frac{f(x)}{\sin ^2 \pi x}=\lim _{x \rightarrow 1} \frac{(\ln (1+(x-1)))^2}{2 \pi^2(x-1)^2}=\frac{1}{2 \pi^2} \\ \Rightarrow \quad\left[\frac{1}{\pi L}\right] & =2 \pi\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Let $f: R \rightarrow R$ defined by $f(x)=\sin x+x$ if $\int_0^\pi f^{-1}(x) d x=\frac{\pi^2}{2}-k$, then the value of $k$ is
questions.solution:
$\int_0^\pi f^{-1}(x) d x=\int_0^\pi t f^{\prime}(t) d t$
$\begin{aligned} & =[t f(t)]_0^\pi-\int_0^\pi(t+\sin t) d t \\ & =\pi^2-\frac{\pi^2}{2}-1-1 \\ & =\frac{\pi^2}{2}-2 \\ & \Rightarrow k=2\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Let $I=\int_{\frac{-\sqrt{3}}{2}}^{\frac{\sqrt{3}}{2}} \sqrt{\frac{1-x}{1+x}} \sin ^{-1} x d x$. If $I=\frac{\pi}{M}-\sqrt{N}$, then find the value of $(M+N)$.
questions.solution:
$$
I=\int_{\frac{-\sqrt{3}}{2}}^{\frac{\sqrt{3}}{2}} \sqrt{\frac{1-x}{1+x}} \sin ^{-1} x d x=\int_{\frac{-\sqrt{3}}{2}}^{\frac{\sqrt{3}}{2}} \frac{1-x}{\sqrt{1-x^2}} \sin ^{-1} x d x
$$
Put $x=\sin \theta$
$I=\int_{\frac{-\pi}{3}}^{\frac{\pi}{3}}(1-\sin \theta) \theta d \theta$ $=\underbrace{\int_{\frac{-\pi}{3}}^{\frac{\pi}{3}} \theta d \theta}_{\text {odd, } \therefore 0}-\int_{\frac{-\pi}{3}}^{\frac{\pi}{3}} \theta \sin \theta d \theta$ $=-\int_0^{\frac{\pi}{3}} \theta \sin \theta d \theta$
$\begin{aligned} & I=-2\left[-\left.\theta \cos \theta\right|_0 ^{\pi / 3}+\int_0^{\frac{\pi}{3}} \cos \theta d \theta\right] \\ & I=-2\left[\frac{-\pi}{6}+\frac{\sqrt{3}}{2}\right]=\frac{\pi}{3}-\sqrt{3} \equiv \frac{\pi}{M}-\sqrt{N}\end{aligned}$
$$
\therefore \quad(M+N)=6
$$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: The value of integral $\int_{1 / e}^{\tan x} \frac{t}{1+t^2} d t+\int_{/ / e}^{\cot x} \frac{d t}{t\left(1+t^2\right)}$ is equal to
questions.solution:
$\begin{aligned}
& \mathrm{I}=\int_{\mathrm{t}_0}^{\tan x} \frac{\mathrm{t}}{1+\mathrm{t}^2} \mathrm{dt}+\int_{1_0}^{\cot x} \frac{\mathrm{dt}}{\mathrm{t}\left(1+\mathrm{t}^2\right)} \\
& \text { Put } \mathrm{t}=\frac{1}{\mathrm{z}} \Rightarrow \mathrm{dt}=\frac{-1}{\mathrm{z}^2} \mathrm{~d} \text {. } \\
& I=\int_{1 / 0}^{\tan x} \frac{1}{1+t^2} d t+\int_e^{\operatorname{lan} x} \frac{-1}{\frac{z^2}{z}\left(1+\frac{1}{z^2}\right)} d z \\
& \Rightarrow \mathrm{I}=\int_{\mathrm{V} / \mathrm{c}}^{\tan \mathrm{x}} \frac{\mathrm{t}}{1+\mathrm{t}^2} \mathrm{dt}-\int_{\mathrm{e}}^{\tan \mathrm{x}} \frac{\mathrm{z}}{\mathrm{z}^2+1} \mathrm{dz} \\
& \Rightarrow I=\int_{1 / e}^{\tan x} \frac{t}{1+t^2} d t+\int_{\tan x}^e \frac{1}{1+t^2} d t \\
& \left.\Rightarrow \mathrm{l}=\int_{1 / \mathrm{e}}^e \frac{1}{1+\mathrm{t}^2} \mathrm{dt}=\frac{1}{2} \ln \left(1+\mathrm{t}^2\right)\right)_{1 \mathrm{c}}^{\mathrm{e}} \\
& =\frac{1}{2}\left[\ln \left(1+\mathrm{e}^2\right)-\ln \left(1+\frac{1}{\mathrm{e}^2}\right)\right] \\
& \Rightarrow \mathrm{I}=\frac{1}{2}[2]=1
\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q:
$R-\{0\} \rightarrow R$ is a differentiable function such that:
$$
\int_1^{x y} f(t) d t=y \int_1^x f(t) d t+x \int_1^y f(t) d t \forall x, y, \in(R)-\{0\} \text { and } f(1)=1 .
$$

Function $g$ is defined as:
$$
g(x)=-\left(e^{f\left(x^2\right)-1}+\left(e^{f\left(1 / x^2\right)-1}\right)\right)
$$

If $I(P)=\int_P^{1 / P} e^{g(x)} d x$ then, the value of $\lim _{P \rightarrow 0^{+}} I(P)=\frac{\sqrt{a}}{b}(a$ and $b \in R)$. Find the value of $[a+b]$.
(You may use $\int_{-\infty}^{\infty} e^{-t^2} d t=\sqrt{\pi}$ )
[Note: $[k]$ denotes greatest integer function less than or equal to $k$.]
questions.solution:
$$
\int_1^{x y} f(t) d t=y \int_1^\pi f(t) d t+x \int_1^y f(t) d t \forall x, y, \in(R)-\{0\} \text { and } f(1)=1
$$

Differentiate both sides w.r.t. $x$
$$
y f(x y)=y f(x)+\int_1^y f(t) d t
$$


Put $x=1$, we get
$$
y f(y)=y+\int_1^y f(t) d t
$$ $(\because f(1)=1)$


Again differentiate w.r.t. $y$
$$
\begin{aligned}
& & f(y)+y f^{\prime}(y) & =1+f(y) \\
\Rightarrow & & y f^{\prime}(y) & =1 \\
\Rightarrow & & f^{\prime}(y) & =\frac{1}{y}
\end{aligned}
$$

Hence, $f(y)=1+\ln y$
$$
\therefore \quad g(x)=-\left(x^2+\frac{1}{x^2}\right)
$$

Now, we have to find
$$
I=\int_0^{\infty} e^{-\left(x^2+\frac{1}{x^2}\right)} d x
$$ ...(1)
Replace $x \rightarrow \frac{1}{x}$
$$
\begin{aligned}
I & =\int_0^{\infty} e^{-\left(x^2+\frac{1}{x^2}\right)} \frac{1}{x^2} d x \\
2 I & =\int_0^{\infty} e^{-\left(x^2+\frac{1}{x^2}\right)}\left(1+\frac{1}{x^2}\right) d x \\
2 I & =\int_0^{\infty} e^{-\left(x-\frac{1}{x}\right)-2}\left(1+\frac{1}{x^2}\right) d x \\
\Rightarrow \quad 2 e^2 I & =\int_0^{\infty} e^{-\left(x-\frac{1}{x}\right)^2}\left(1+\frac{1}{x^2}\right) d x
\end{aligned}
$$
$\begin{aligned} & \operatorname{Put}\left(x-\frac{1}{x}\right)=t \\ & \Rightarrow \quad 2 e^2 I=\int_{-\infty}^{\infty} e^{-t^2} d t=2 \int_{-\infty}^{\infty} e^{-t^2} d t \\ & \text { Hence, } \quad e^2 I=\int_{-\infty}^{\infty} e^{-t^2} d t=\sqrt{\pi} \\ & \therefore \quad I=\frac{\sqrt{\pi}}{e^2}=\frac{\sqrt{a}}{b} \\ & \Rightarrow \quad[a+b]=\left[\pi+e^2\right]=10 \\ & \end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Let $I(n)=\int_0^\pi \ln \left(1-2 n \cos x+n^2\right) d x$. Find the value of $\frac{I(100)}{I(10)}$.
questions.solution:
$$
I(n)=\int_0^\pi \ln \left(1-2 n \cos x+n^2\right) d x
$$

Using King and add
$$
I(n)=\frac{1}{2} \int_0^\pi \ln \left(1+n^4-2 n^2 \cos 2 x\right) d x
$$
[Put $2 x=t]$
$$
I(n)=\frac{1}{4} \int_0^{2 \pi} \ln \left(t+n^4-2 n^2 \cos t\right) d t
$$

Using Queen
$$
\begin{aligned}
& I(n)=\frac{1}{2} \int_0^\pi \ln \left(1+n^4-2 n^2 \cos t\right) d t=\frac{1}{2} I\left(n^2\right) \\
& \therefore \quad \frac{I\left(n^2\right)}{I(n)}=2 \text {; } \\
& \text { Hence, } \frac{I(100)}{I(10)}=2 \\
&
\end{aligned}
$$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q:
Consider a function $f(x)$ which satisfies $f^{\prime}(x)=\tan ^2 x+\int_{\frac{-\pi}{4}}^{\frac{\pi}{4}} f(x) d x$. Also $f\left(\frac{\pi}{4}\right)=\frac{-\pi}{4}$.
If the value of $\frac{8+\pi^2}{\pi} \int_{\frac{-\pi}{4}}^{\frac{\pi}{4}} f(x) d x=m$, then find the value of $m^2$.
questions.solution:
$$
f^{\prime}(x)=\tan ^2 x+K \text { where } K=\int_{\frac{-\pi}{4}}^{\frac{\pi}{4}} f(x) d x
$$
$$
\begin{aligned}
& f(x)=\tan x-x+K x+C \\
& f\left(\frac{\pi}{4}\right)=1-\frac{\pi}{4}+\frac{K \pi}{4}+C=\frac{-\pi}{4}
\end{aligned}
$$
$$
C+1=\frac{-K \pi}{4}
$$
$$
f(x)=\tan x-x+K x-\frac{K \pi}{4}-1
$$

Now,
$$
K=\int_{\frac{-\pi}{4}}^{\frac{\pi}{4}}(\underbrace{\tan x-x+K x}_{\text {odd function }}-\frac{K \pi}{4}-1) d x=\frac{-\pi}{2}-\frac{K \pi^2}{8}
$$

Hence, $K=\frac{-4 \pi}{8+\pi^2}$
$$
\therefore \frac{8+\pi^2}{\pi} \cdot \int_{\frac{-\pi}{4}}^{\frac{\pi}{4}} f(x) d x=-4 \equiv m \Rightarrow m^2=16
$$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: If $\int_0^\pi \frac{\sin x(\sin x+1) e^{\sin x+\cos x}}{e^{\cos x}+1} d x=a+b \int_0^\pi e^{\sin x} d x$ where $a$ and $b$ are positive rational numbers, then find the value of $100\left(a^2+b^2\right)$.
questions.solution:
Using King
$$
\begin{aligned}
& I=\int_0^\pi \frac{\sin x(1+\sin x) e^{\sin x-\cos x}}{e^{-\cos x}+1} d x \\
& I=\int_0^\pi \frac{\sin x(1+\sin x) e^{\sin x}}{e^{\cos x}+1} d x
\end{aligned}
$$ ...(1)
Add
$$
\begin{aligned}
& 2 I=\int_0^\pi \frac{\sin x(1+\sin x) e^{\sin x}\left(e^{\cos x}+1\right)}{e^{\cos x}+1} d x \\
& I=\frac{1}{2} \int_0^\pi \sin x(1+\sin x) e^{\sin x} d x \\
& I=\frac{1}{2} \int_0^\pi e^{\sin x}\left(\sin x+1-\cos ^2 x\right) d x \\
& I=\frac{1}{2} \int_0^\pi e^{\sin x} d x+\frac{1}{2} \int_0^\pi e^{\sin x}\left(\sin x-\cos ^2 x\right) d x \\
& I=\frac{1}{2} \int_0^\pi e^{\sin x} d x+\frac{1}{2}\left[e^{\sin x}(-\cos x)\right]_0^\pi \\
& I=\frac{1}{2} \int_0^\pi e^{\sin x} d x+\frac{1}{2}[1+1] \\
& I=1+\frac{1}{2} \int_0^\pi e^{\sin x} d x \\
& \therefore \quad 100\left(1+\frac{1}{4}\right)=125 \\
&
\end{aligned}
$$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q:
If $\left[\begin{array}{lll}1 & 0 & 0 \\ 6 & 2 & 0 \\ 5 & 4 & 3\end{array}\right]\left[\begin{array}{c}\mathrm{x} \\ \mathrm{x}^2 \\ 1\end{array}\right]=\left[\begin{array}{c}\mathrm{x} \\ 2 \mathrm{ax}+\mathrm{bx} \\ 5 \mathrm{x}+\mathrm{cx}^2+3\end{array}\right] \forall \mathrm{x} \in \mathrm{R}$ and $f(\mathrm{x})$ is a differentiable function satisfying $f(\mathrm{x})+f$
$(\mathrm{y})=f\left(\frac{\mathrm{x}+\mathrm{y}}{1-\mathrm{xy}}\right)$ for all $\mathrm{x}, \mathrm{y} \in \mathrm{R},(\mathrm{xy} \neq 1)$ and $\lim _{\mathrm{x} \rightarrow 0} \frac{f(\mathrm{x})}{\mathrm{x}}=2$, then find the value of $\left[\int_0^1 \frac{\mathrm{ax} \mathrm{x}^2+\mathrm{bx}+\mathrm{c}}{f(1)} \mathrm{dx}\right]$, where [.] denotes greatest integer function.
questions.solution:
$\left.\begin{array}{l}
6 \mathrm{x}+2 \mathrm{x}^2=2 \mathrm{ax}+\mathrm{bx}^2 \\
5 \mathrm{x}+4 \mathrm{x}^2+3=5 \mathrm{x}+\mathrm{cx}^2+3
\end{array}\right\} \Rightarrow \mathrm{a}=3, \mathrm{~b}=2 \& \mathrm{c}=4$

Given functional relation is
$\begin{aligned}
& f(x)+f(y)=f\left(\frac{\mathrm{x}+\mathrm{y}}{1-\mathrm{xy}}\right) \\
& \mathrm{x}=\mathrm{y}=0, f(0)=0 \\
& \mathrm{y}=-\mathrm{x} \\
& f(\mathrm{x})+f(-\mathrm{x})=f(0) \\
& f(\mathrm{x})=-f(-\mathrm{x})
\end{aligned}$
$\begin{aligned}
& \text { Now, } f^{\prime}(x)=\lim _{h \rightarrow 0} \frac{f(x+h)-f(x)}{h} \\
& =\lim _{h \rightarrow 0} \frac{f(x+h)+f(-x)}{h} \\
& =\lim _{h \rightarrow 0} \frac{f\left(\frac{\mathrm{x}+\mathrm{h}-\mathrm{x}}{1-(\mathrm{x}+\mathrm{h})(-\mathrm{x})}\right)}{\mathrm{h}}=\lim _{\mathrm{h} \rightarrow 0} \frac{f\left(\frac{\mathrm{~h}}{1+\mathrm{x}(\mathrm{x}+\mathrm{h})}\right)}{\mathrm{h}} \\
& =\lim _{\mathrm{h} \rightarrow 0} \frac{f\left(\frac{\mathrm{~h}}{1+\mathrm{x}(\mathrm{x}+\mathrm{h})}\right)}{\left(\frac{\mathrm{h}}{1+\mathrm{x}(\mathrm{x}+\mathrm{h})}\right)} \times\left(\frac{1}{1+\mathrm{x}(\mathrm{x}+\mathrm{h})}\right) \\
& f^{\prime}(x)=\frac{2}{1+x^2} \quad\left\{\because \quad \lim _{x \rightarrow 0} \frac{f(x)}{x}=2\right\} \\
& f(\mathrm{x})=2 \tan ^{-1} \mathrm{x}+\mathrm{c} \\
& f(0)=0+c \Rightarrow c=0 \quad\{\because f(0)=0\} \\
& \therefore \quad f(\mathrm{x})=2 \tan ^{-1} \mathrm{x} \\
& \int_0^1 \frac{a x^2+b x+c}{f(1)} d x=\int_0^1 \frac{3 x^2+2 x+4}{2 \cdot \pi / 4} d x \\
& =\frac{2}{\pi}(1+1+4)=\frac{12}{\pi} \\
& \therefore \quad\left[\frac{12}{\pi}\right]=3
\end{aligned}$

questions.q: Let $f(x)$ be monotonically strictly increasing function in $[3,5]$ such that $\int_3^5 f^2(x) d x=9$; $f(1)=3 ; f(4)=5$. Find the value of $2 \int_1^4 x\left(5-f^{-1}(x)\right) d x$

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: The value of $\int_0^{\frac{\pi}{4}} e^{\sec x} \frac{\sin \left(x+\frac{\pi}{4}\right)}{(1-\sin x) \cos x} d x$ can be expressed as $\frac{(a+\sqrt{b}) e^{\sqrt{c}}-e}{\sqrt{d}}$, then find the value of $(a+b+c+d)$.
questions.solution:
$$
I=\frac{1}{\sqrt{2}} \int_0^{\frac{\pi}{4}} e^{\sec x} \frac{\sin x+\cos x}{(1-\sin x) \cos x} d x
$$

Dividing numerator and denominator by $\cos ^2 x$, we have
$$
\begin{aligned}
I & =\frac{1}{\sqrt{2}} \int_0^{\frac{\pi}{4}} e^{\sec x} \frac{\tan x \sec x+\sec x}{(\sin x-\tan x)} d x \\
\Rightarrow \quad I & =\frac{1}{\sqrt{2}} \int_0^{\frac{\pi}{4}} e^{\sec x}\left(\frac{\tan x \sec x}{(\sec x-\tan x)}+\frac{\sec x}{(\sec x-\tan x)}\right) d x \\
\Rightarrow \quad I & =\frac{1}{\sqrt{2}} \int_0^{\frac{\pi}{4}} e^{\sec x}\left(\tan x \sec x\left(\frac{1}{(\sec x-\tan x)}\right)+\left(\frac{\sec x(\sec x-\tan x)}{(\sec x-\tan x)^2}\right)\right) d x \\
\Rightarrow \quad f(x) & =\frac{1}{\sec x-\tan x} \\
I & =\left.\frac{1}{\sqrt{2}} \frac{e^{\sec x}}{(\sec x-\tan x)}\right|_0 ^{\frac{\pi}{4}}
\end{aligned}
$$

Putting the limits, we have
$$
I=\frac{1}{\sqrt{2}}\left(\frac{e^{\sqrt{2}}}{\sqrt{2}-1}-e\right)=\frac{(1+\sqrt{2}) e^{\sqrt{2}}-e}{\sqrt{2}}
$$
which gives, $\quad a=1 ; b=2 ; c=2 ; d=2$
$$
\Rightarrow \quad a+b+c+d=1+2+2+2=7
$$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Let $J_n=\int_0^{\pi / 2}(1-\sin x)^n \sin 2 x d x$. Find $\sum_{n=0}^{\infty} J_n$.
questions.solution:
Put $\sin x=t$
$$
\begin{aligned}
J_n & =\int_0^1 t(1-t)^n d t=2(\int_0^1(1-t)^n d t-\int_0^1 \underbrace{(1-t)(1-t)^n}_{(1-t)^{n+1}} d t) \\
& =2\left(\int_0^1 t^n d t-\int_0^1 t^{n+1} d t\right)=2\left(\frac{1}{n+1}-\frac{1}{n+2}\right)
\end{aligned}
$$
$\begin{aligned} \therefore \quad \sum_{n=0}^{\infty} J_n & =2 \sum_{n=0}^{\infty}\left(\frac{1}{n+1}-\frac{1}{n+2}\right) \\ & =2(1)=2\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Let $I_1=\int_1^3\left(x^2+x+3\right) f\left(x^3-2 x^2-5 x+2020\right) d x$ and $I_2=\int_1^3\left(4 x^2-3 x-2\right) f\left(x^3-2 x^2-5 x+2020\right) d x$. If the value of $\frac{2 I_1}{3 I_2}=\frac{a}{b}$ where $a$ and $b$ are co-prime, then find the value of $(a+b)$.
questions.solution:
Consider
$$
I_2-I_1=\int_1^3\left(3 x^2-4 x-5\right) f\left(x^3-2 x^2-5 x+2020\right) d x
$$

Put
$$
\begin{aligned}
& \text { Put } & x^3-2 x^2-5 x+2020 & =t \\
& \therefore & \left(3 x^2-4 x-5\right) d x & =d t \\
& \therefore & I_2-I_1 & =\int_{2014}^{2014} f(t) d t=0
\end{aligned}
$$

Hence,
$$
\begin{aligned}
I_2 & =I_1 \\
\frac{2 I_1}{3 I_2} & =\frac{2}{3} \equiv \frac{a}{b} \Rightarrow a+b=5
\end{aligned}
$$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: The value of the definite integral $\int_0^{\pi / 2} \sin ^2 t \cdot \ln (\sin t) d t$ can be expressed as $\frac{\pi^a}{b}(c-\ln d)$, where $a, b, c$ and $d$ are integers. Find the smallest possible value of $a+b+c+d$.
questions.solution:
$I=\int_0^{\pi / 2} \sin ^2 t \ln (\sin t) d t=\int_0^{\pi / 2} \frac{1}{2}(1-\cos 2 t) \ln (\sin t) d t$
$$
\left.=\frac{1}{2}\left(t-\frac{\sin 2 t}{2}\right) \ln (\sin t)\right]_0^{\pi / 2}-\frac{1}{2} \int_0^{\pi / 2} t \cot t d t+\frac{1}{4} \int_0^{\pi / 2} \sin 2 t \cot t d t
$$
(Using I.B.P.)
$\left.=0-\frac{1}{2} t \ln \sin t\right]_0^{\pi / 2}+\frac{1}{2} \int_0^{\pi / 2} \ln \sin t d t+\frac{1}{2} \int_0^{\pi / 2} \cos ^2 t d t$
$$
=0-0-\frac{\pi}{4} \ln 2+\frac{1}{4} \int_0^{\pi / 2}(1+\cos 2 t) d t
$$
[Note: $\left.\int_0^{\pi / 2} \ln (\sin x) d x=\frac{-\pi}{2} \ln 2\right]$
$$
=-\frac{\pi}{4} \ln 2+\frac{1}{4}\left[t+\frac{\sin 2 t}{2}\right]_0^{\pi / 2}=-\frac{\pi}{4} \ln 2+\frac{\pi}{8}=\frac{\pi}{8}(1-\ln 4)
$$

Therefore, $a+b+c+d=1+8+1+4=14$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: If $\int_0^{1 / 2} \tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{1}{2}\left(\sqrt{\frac{1+x}{x}}-\sqrt{\frac{x}{1+x}}\right)\right) d x=p \ln (2+\sqrt{3})-\frac{\pi}{q}$, then $p q$ is equal to:
questions.solution:
$$
\begin{aligned}
& \int_0^{1 / 2} \tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{1}{2}\left(\sqrt{\frac{1+x}{x}}-\sqrt{\frac{x}{1+x}}\right)\right) d x=\int_0^{1 / 2} \tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{1}{2}\left(\frac{1}{\sqrt{x(1+x)}}\right)\right) d x \\
& =\int_0^{1 / 2} \tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{1}{\sqrt{4 x^2+4 x+1-1}}\right) d x=\int_0^{1 / 2} \tan ^{-1}\left(\frac{1}{\sqrt{(2 x+1)^2-1}}\right) d x
\end{aligned}
$$
Put $2 x+1=\sec \theta \Rightarrow d x=\frac{\sec \theta \tan \theta}{2} d \theta$
$$
\begin{aligned}
I & =\int_0^{\frac{\pi}{3}} \tan ^{-1}(\cos \theta) \cdot \frac{\sec \theta \tan \theta}{2} d \theta=\int_0^{\frac{\pi}{3}}\left(\frac{\pi}{2}-\theta\right) \cdot \frac{\sec \theta \tan \theta}{2} d \theta \\
I & =\frac{\pi}{4} \cdot(\sec \theta)_0^{\pi / 3}-\frac{1}{2}(\theta \cdot \sec \theta-\ln (\sec \theta+\tan \theta))_0^{\pi / 3} \\
& =\frac{\pi}{4}(2-1)-\frac{1}{2}\left(\frac{\pi}{2} \cdot 2-\ln (2+\sqrt{3})\right)=\frac{-\pi}{12}+\frac{\ln (2+\sqrt{3})}{2} \equiv p \ln (2+\sqrt{3})-\frac{\pi}{q}
\end{aligned}
$$
$$
\therefore \quad p q=6
$$

$$

questions.q: If $f$ and $g$ are two functions such that $2 f(1)=g(2)=4$ and $2 f(9)=g(10)=20$ and $\int_0^2\left(x^2 g\left(f\left(x^3+1\right)\right) f^{\prime}\left(x^3+1\right)-3 x^2\right) d x=0$, then find the value of $\int_4^{20} g^{-1}(x) d x$.
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} & f\left(x^3+1\right)=t \Rightarrow f^{\prime}\left(x^3+1\right) \cdot 3 x^2 d x=d t \\ \Rightarrow & \frac{1}{3} \int_2^{10} g(t) d t-\left(\frac{3 \cdot x^3}{3}\right)_0^2=0 \\ \Rightarrow & \int_2^{10} g(t) d t=24 \Rightarrow \int_2^{10} g(x) d x+\int_4^{20} g^{-1}(x) d x=200-8 \\ \Rightarrow & \int_4^{20} g^{-1}(x) d x=200-8-24=168\end{aligned}$

questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.q:
If $\int_{-n}^n \frac{3\{x\}+1}{\{3 x\}+1} d x=6 \ln \left(4 e^2\right)$, then find the value of $n$.
[Note: $\{k\}$ denotes the fractional part function of $k$.]
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} I_n & =2 n \int_0^1 \frac{3\{x\}+1}{\{3 x\}+1} d x=2 n\left(\int_0^{\frac{1}{3}} \frac{3 x+1}{3 x+1} d x+\int_{\frac{1}{3}}^{\frac{2}{3}} \frac{3 x+1}{3 x-1+1} d x+\int_{\frac{2}{3}}^1 \frac{3 x+1}{3 x-2+1} d x\right) \\ & =2 n\left(\frac{1}{3}+\frac{1}{3}+\frac{\ln 2}{3}+\int_{\frac{2}{3}}^1\left(1+\frac{2}{3 x-1}\right) d x\right)=2 n\left(\frac{1}{3}+\frac{1}{3}+\frac{\ln 2}{3}+\frac{1}{3}+\frac{2}{3}(\ln (3 x-1))_{\frac{2}{3}}^1\right) \\ & =2 n\left(1+\frac{\ln 2}{3}+\frac{2 \ln 2}{3}\right)=2 n(1+\ln 2)=n \ln \left(4 e^2\right) \equiv 6 \ln \left(4 e^2\right) \\ \therefore n & =6\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q:
$$
\left|2\left(x^2+\frac{1}{x^2}\right)+\right| 1-x^2||=4\left(\frac{3}{2}-2^{x^2-3}-\frac{1}{2^{x^2+1}}\right)
$$
If $x_1$ and $x_2$, where $x_1 < x_2$, are two values of $x$ satisfying the equation above, find the value of $\int_{x_1-x_2}^{3 x_2-x_1}\left\{\frac{x}{4}\right\}\left(1+\left[\tan \left(\frac{\{x\}}{1+\{x\}}\right)\right]\right) d x$
[Note: $|\cdot|$ denotes the absolute value function,
\{.\} denotes the fraction part function,
[-] denotes the floor function]
questions.solution:
Since the equation is even on both sides,we only need to consider $x \geq d$ to find the positive solution $x_2$ and then $x_1=-x_2$. For $0 \leq x \leq 1$,
$$
\begin{aligned}
2\left(x^2+\frac{1}{x^2}\right)+1-x^2 & =4\left(\frac{3}{2}-2^{x^2-1}-\frac{1}{2^{x^2-1}}\right) \\
x^2+\frac{2}{x^2}+1 & =6-2^{x^2-1}-\frac{1}{2^{x^2-1}} \\
x^2+\frac{2}{x^2}+2^{x^2-1}+\frac{1}{2^{x^2-1}} & =5
\end{aligned}
$$


By inspection, the solution is $x^2=1$ or $x_1=-1$ and $x^2=1$, then
$$
\begin{aligned}
I & =\int_0^4\left\{\frac{x}{4}\right\}\left(1+\left[\tan \left(\frac{\{x\}}{1+\{x\}}\right)\right] d x \quad \text { Note that }\left[\tan \left(\frac{\{x\}}{1+\{x\}}\right)\right]=0\right. \\
& =\int_0^4\left\{\frac{x}{4}\right\} d x=\int_0^4 \frac{x}{4} d x=\left.\frac{x^2}{8}\right|_0 ^4=2
\end{aligned}
$$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q:
For some function $f(x)$ and $g(x)$ which are differentiable $\forall x>0$ satisfy the following condition.
(i) $\left(\frac{f(x)}{x}\right)^{\prime}=x^2 e^{-x^2}$
(ii) $g(x)=\frac{4}{e^4} \int_1^x e^{t^2} \cdot f(t) d t$
(iii) $f(1)=\frac{1}{e}$
Find the value of $3 e^4(f(2)-g(2))$.
questions.solution:
$\begin{aligned} g(x) & =\frac{2}{e^4} \int_1^x \underbrace{2 t e^{t^2}}_{\mathrm{II}} \underbrace{f(t)}_{\mathrm{I}} d t=\frac{2}{e^4}\left(\left.\frac{f(t)}{t} \cdot e^{t^2}\right|_1 ^x-\int_1^x\left(\frac{f(t)}{t}\right) e^{t^2} d t\right) \\ & =\frac{2}{e^4}\left(\frac{f(x)}{x} \cdot e^{x^2}-1-\int_1^x t^2 d t\right)=\frac{2}{e^4}\left(\frac{f(x)}{x} \cdot e^{x^2}-1-\frac{1}{3}\left(x^3-1\right)\right)\end{aligned}$
$\begin{aligned} g(x)=\frac{2}{e^4}\left(\frac{f(2)}{2} \cdot e^4-1-\frac{8}{3}+\frac{1}{3}\right)=f(2)-\frac{20}{3 e^4} \\ \Rightarrow(f(2)-g(2)) 3 e^4=20\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Let $k(x)$ be a continuous function satisfying the equation $\int_0^{x^3} k(t) d t=x^{1+x^2}$, find the value of $3 k(1)$.
questions.solution:
By Leibnitz Theorem, $\frac{d}{d x} \int_0^{x^3} k(t) d t=\frac{d}{d x}\left(x^{1+x^2}\right)$
$$
k\left(x^3\right) \cdot 3 x^2-0=\left(1+x^2\right) x^{x^2}+x^{1+x^2}(\ln x) \cdot(2 x)
$$

Put $x=1 \Rightarrow k(1) \cdot 3=2 \cdot 1+1 \cdot 0 \cdot 2$
$$
k(1)=\frac{2}{3} \Rightarrow 3 k(1)=2
$$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Let $f$ be a differentiable function defined in $[0,1]$ such that $f(f(x))=x$ and $f(0)=1$. If the value of $\int_0^1(x-f(x))^{2018} d x=\frac{p}{q}$ where $p$ and $q$ are relatively prime then find the value of $(p+q)$.
questions.solution:
$$ 
I=\int_0^1(x-f(x))^{2018} d x 
...(1)$$ 
Putting,
$$ 
\begin{aligned}
& x=f(t) \Rightarrow d x=f^{\prime}(t) d t \\
& I=\int_1^0(f(t)-f(f(t)))^{2018} f^{\prime}(t) d t \quad\{\because f(f(0))=0 \Rightarrow f(1)=0\} \\
& I=-\int_0^1(f(t)-t)^{2018} f^{\prime}(t) d t \\
& I=-\int_0^1(t-f(t))^{2018} f^{\prime}(t) d t  .....(2)\end{aligned}$$

Eqn. (1) + Eqn. (2)
$$
\begin{aligned}
2 I & =\int_0^1(x-f(x))^{2018}\left(1-f^{\prime}(x)\right) d x=\left(\frac{(x-f(x))^{2019}}{2019}\right)_0^1 \\
& =\frac{1}{2019}-\left(\frac{-1}{2019}\right) \\
I & =\frac{1}{2019} \equiv \frac{p}{q} \Rightarrow(p+q)=2020
\end{aligned}
$$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Let $f$ be a continuous and even function such that $\int_0^a f(x) d x=10$. If $g(x)$ is a continuous positive function such that $g(x) g(-x)=1$ and $\int_0^a g(x) d x=5$, then find the value of $\int_{-a}^a \frac{f(x)}{1+g(x)} d x$
questions.solution:
$\begin{aligned} f(-x) & =f(x), g(x) g(-x)=1 \\ \int_0^a f(x) d x & =10\end{aligned}$
$I=\int_{-a}^a \frac{f(x)}{1+g(x)} d x$ ...(1)
Using King $\quad I=\int_{-a}^a \frac{f(-x)}{1+g(-x)} d x$
$\Rightarrow \quad I=\int_{-a}^a \frac{g(x) f(x) d x}{1+g(x)}$ ...(2)
Eqn. (1) + Eqn. (2)
$$
\Rightarrow \quad 2 I=\int_{-a}^a f(x) d x=2 \int_0^a f(x) d x \Rightarrow I=\int_0^a f(x) d x=10
$$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q:
Let $f:(0,1) \rightarrow R$ be a function defined by $f(x)=10 x^{\sin x+\cos x}$. If $I=\int_0^1 f(x) d x$, then find the value of $[I]$.
[Note: Where $[k]$ denotes the greatest integer function less than or equal to $k$.]
questions.solution:
$$
\sin x+\cos x=\sqrt{2} \cos \left(x-\frac{\pi}{4}\right) \in[1, \sqrt{2}] \forall x \in[0,1]
$$

Hence, as $\quad x \in[0,1]$
$$
10 x^{\sqrt{2}} \leq f(x) \leq 10 x
$$

With equality holding if and only if $x=0$ or 1
As equality holds only for finitely many points, the inequalities become on strict integrating on all sides.
Hence, $\quad 4 < 10(\sqrt{2}-1) < \int_0^1 f(x) d x < 5 \Rightarrow\left[\int_0^1 f(x) d x\right]=4$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Let $f(x)$ be a function defined on $R$ such that $f^{\prime}(x)=f^{\prime}(3-x) \forall x \in[0,3]$ with $f(0)=-32$ and $f(3)=$ 46 , then find the value of $\frac{1}{3} \int_0^3 f(x) d x$ $\qquad$
questions.solution:
We have $f^{\prime}(x)=f^{\prime}(3-x)$ on integrating w.r.t. $x$
$\begin{aligned}
& f(x)=-f(3-x)+c \\
& \text { Put } x=0 \\
& f(0)+f(3)=c \\
& -32+46=c \\
& c=14
\end{aligned}$
Now let $I=\int_0^3 f(x) d x....(i)$
$I=\int_0^3 f(3-x) d x.....(ii)$
Adding (1) and (2), we get $2 \mathrm{I}=\int_0^3(f(x)+f(3-x)) d x=\int_0^3 14 d x=42$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Let $f(x)$ be a continuous, periodic and bounded function with period 3 such that $\int_0^3 f(t) d t=6$. Also $g^{\prime}(x)=f(x)$, such that $g(0)=0$. Find the value of $\lim _{x \rightarrow 0} x g\left(\frac{1}{x}\right)$,
questions.solution:
$$
\begin{aligned}
& \int_0^x g^{\prime}(x) d x=\int_0^x f(x) d x \\
& g(x)=\int_0^x f(t) d t \\
& \lim _{x \rightarrow 0} x g\left(\frac{1}{x}\right) \text { Put } x=\frac{1}{t} \\
& \lim _{t \rightarrow \infty} \frac{g(t)}{t} \\
& \lim _{t \rightarrow \infty} \frac{\int_0^t f(x) d x}{t}
\end{aligned}
$$
et $t=n a$ where $n \in I a$ is period $=3$
$$
\lim _{t \rightarrow \infty} \frac{\int_0^{n a} f(t) d t}{n a}=\frac{n \int_0^3 f(x) d x}{3 n}=\frac{6}{3}=2
$$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Mathematics
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: If $I_r=\int_a^{\pi+a}\left|\frac{1}{r} \sin x+\frac{1}{r+1} \cos x\right| d x$ where $a \in R$ and $r \in N$, then $\lim _{n \rightarrow \infty} \sum_{r=1}^n\left(I_r^2-\frac{8}{(r+1)^2}\right)$equals:
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} I_r & =2 \sqrt{\frac{1}{r^2}+\frac{1}{(r+1)^2}} \\ I_r^2 & =\frac{4}{r^2}+\frac{4}{(r+1)^2} \\ I_r^2-\frac{8}{(r+1)^2} & =\frac{4}{r^2}-\frac{4}{(r+1)^2} \\ \sum_{r=1}^n\left(I_r^2-\frac{8}{(r+1)^2}\right) & =4\left(\frac{1}{1^2}-\frac{1}{(r+1)^2}\right) \\ \therefore \quad \lim _{n \rightarrow \infty} \sum_{r=1}^n\left(I_r^2-\frac{8}{(r+1)^2}\right) & =4\end{aligned}$

questions.q: If the value of $\lim _{n \rightarrow \infty} \sum_{k=0}^{n-1} \frac{k}{n}\left[\sqrt[m]{\frac{k+1}{n}}-\sqrt[m]{\frac{k}{n}}\right],(m \in N)$ is equal to $\frac{1}{10}$ then find the value of $m$.
